steps.title: Read the frequencies and bin ranges from both histograms
steps.content:
Both data sets have the same bar heights (3, 4, 7, 9) for a total of 23 values each, but the bin labels are shifted:
  • Data Set A bins: [20, 30), [30, 40), [40, 50), [50, 60)
  • Data Set B bins: [10, 20), [20, 30), [30, 40), [40, 50)
Every Set A bin sits exactly 10 units higher than the corresponding Set B bin.

steps.title: Make the two sums as close as possible
steps.content:
Values are integers, and within each bin we may pick any integer from the lower bound up to (upper bound − 1). To minimize $|\text{sum}_A - \text{sum}_B|$, choose the smallest integer in every Set A bin and the largest integer in every Set B bin:
$$\text{sum}_A^{\min} = 3(20)+4(30)+7(40)+9(50) = 910$$
$$\text{sum}_B^{\max} = 3(19)+4(29)+7(39)+9(49) = 887$$
Their difference is $910 - 887 = 23$, and the sums cannot be made equal because every Set A value is at least 10 more than its matching Set B value.

steps.title: Convert to a mean difference
steps.content: Each data set has 23 values, so the smallest possible difference of means is $$\frac{|\text{sum}_A - \text{sum}_B|}{23} = \frac{23}{23} = 1.$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.
